summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend e2e testing of ClanChat. 30/31 pytest tests passed (1 skipped is expected behavior — adult cannot resolve minor's handle, which is the minor-protection working as intended). All frontend flows verified: login, feed, compose with all tiers, NSFW conditional visibility, post creation with tags, like toggle, search, user profile, /me tabs, settings, light/dark theme. Fixed one MongoDB ObjectId serialization 500 bug in POST /api/boards and POST /api/boards/{id}/messages.

minor.issue: Returned 500 because Mongo's insert_one mutated doc adding _id ObjectId, then returned doc directly — FastAPI failed to serialize ObjectId. Fixed during this run by popping _id before return.

action_items: Optionally verify the boards _id fix on other endpoints (search.py spot-check showed list endpoints already use projection {_id:0}; only the two POST handlers were affected).
Consider exposing a way for adults to identify minors-by-handle that still 404s but with a clearer protection signal — currently silent 404 is fine for privacy.
Pinned-max-3 currently rejects 4th pin with 400 — fine. Consider returning a user-friendly message constant in the response.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py mutates Mongo insert dicts and returns them — risky pattern; recommend always returning a freshly constructed Pydantic/dict response without _id (only two cases found and fixed).

/api/boards/by-user returns {boards:[...]} but /api/users/search returns {results:[...]} and /api/posts/feed returns {posts:[...]} — naming is inconsistent across list endpoints; consider a single envelope shape (e.g., {items, total}).
Login response shape: /api/auth/login returns {user:{...}} while /api/auth/me returns the user object directly — frontend handles both but the asymmetry is a minor footgun.
User id field is `user_id` (not `id`). API contract should document this clearly so client SDKs and tests don't assume `id`.

Posts use `content` field while older drafts/tests used `body` — field name is correct, but Pydantic should reject unknown extras (currently silently accepts payload mismatch leading to 'Post cannot be empty').
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_clanchat.py (rewrote using actual response schemas: user_id, by-handle envelope, content/nsfw field names, post_id, feed envelope, search envelope, boards title vs name, reports category required, minor age >=13)
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_* prefixed accounts and TEST posts during the run. No cleanup performed (test data isolated via TEST_ prefix). Pinned posts created during pin-max-3 test were unpinned in teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend schemas are: login -> {user:{user_id, handle, ...}}; auth/me -> user obj directly; by-handle -> {user, relation}; posts use 'content' field and 'nsfw' (not is_nsfw); feed -> {posts:[]}; search -> {results:[]}; boards -> {boards:[]} with title (not name); reports require 'category' field; minor min age is 13 — use dob 2012 for minor. Cookies are httpOnly samesite=none secure — use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (production https) not localhost. Inner Circle is invite-only, no follow-request endpoint. Wall default permission is owner-only.
rca of the issue: RCA — POST /api/boards 500: server.py:951 builds doc, calls db.boards.insert_one(doc) which mutates doc (Motor adds _id ObjectId in place), then returns doc. FastAPI default JSON encoder cannot serialize bson.ObjectId -> raises 'ValueError: ObjectId object is not iterable'. Mitigation: pop _id before return OR construct a separate response dict. Same pattern existed in /api/boards/{board_id}/messages — both fixed.
